"We didn't call them KKK. We called them clubsters, my family called them the clubsters"; canonical Patton-family-internal nickname for the KKK

"I'm with filmmaker and biographer John Bishop" — Whisper rendered "videographer" (Bishop's canonical role across all SOHP interviews in this corpus) as "biographer" (different word, definitionally wrong)

Same context as P2.21; speaker references a "Twilight"-titled book she recalls as "almost like a Bible to the gay world, male gay"; canonical possibilities include Carl B. Roberts' *Twilight Men* (1931, foundational gay novel) or works in the Mattachine-era publishing tradition; spelling not yet verified

"I learned about Lesbos. I never forget that. And then I learned the clinical term... Lesbian. So I began to lose people through that. So I don't know how I got to this, but coming to SNCC" — canonical 1950s-60s vernacular terms; "Lesbos" is Whisper's rendering of "Lesbians" (probably collapsed phonetics); "Bull Divers" is Whisper for "Bulldykes"; the speaker preserved these as period-vernacular for her own pedagogical purpose
"His name was Junior's name was Robert Harris. They both died so I can call him name" — Patton's cousin Bobby's male partner; preserved as speaker-originating individual reference

pending_context.notes: "she wore these spike high heels. And she had a clickety-clack when she walked. I mean, poise and all of that business" — preserved as speaker-originating description of Patton's Aunt Chick

"and saw these black and white kids or young adults sitting on our sofa, I did not notice that she was disconcerted... she says, Gwendolyn come with me and I go to the kitchen. And I knew she was going to get cookies and lemonade. I mean, that's the nice thing to do. We call them repass" — canonical Southern Black term "repast" (small refreshment served after funerals, etc.); "repass" is Whisper's homophone-substitution
Same as Pass 1 #52.45; preserved as Patton's family-internal term — not a Whisper error
"with my grandmother, my called mommy, on my mother's side"; Patton's family-internal name for her maternal grandmother — not a Whisper error

"Aunt Jix, the Lottie Dot Teachers. And I'm eating there" — Patton's term for her status-conscious schoolteacher aunts (Aunt Chick and Aunt Flora); "Lottie Dot" is Whisper for the idiom "la-di-da" (or similar Black-Southern colloquialism for class-pretensions); speaker-originating period vernacular

pending_context.notes: "And then a youngster, young and then I, Jolacy, was summing downstairs" — Whisper-garbled name of the 14-year-old who crawled through basement windows to make phone calls during the May 1961 First Baptist standoff; preserved as speaker-originating until canonical identification verified

pending_context.whisper: a tin / I had a tin
pending_context.notes: "I had oxygen at that time. I had a tin" — canonical oxygen tent (an enclosed plastic/canvas chamber used in pre-mask era for oxygen therapy); "tin" is Whisper for "tent" (consonant substitution)
"All covered up, masked, head, dough" — Whisper-garbled description of full PPE attire; preserved as ambiguous

"what we would call a sitting room warplowler" — canonical sitting room / parlor; "warplowler" is heavily-garbled Whisper rendering of "[the] parlor" (possibly "warm parlor"); preserved as common-noun garble
"You know, it was, what do you call splendor? There was this huge baby grand piano" — Whisper-garbled rendering of speaker's description; preserved as common-noun
"I had TV, or whatever his thing" — Patton self-references her tuberculosis history; "TV" is Whisper for "TB" (consonant-substitution); recurring substitution in this transcript

"they call North Montgomery, Powertown, Newtown" — canonical historic Black Montgomery neighborhoods; "Powertown" is unverified (possibly "Centennial Hill" or speaker's family-internal nickname); preserving as low-confidence geographic

"The high pollutants became dear friends to Reverend Abernathy" — Whisper-garbled rendering of "high mucky-mucks" (Black-Southern colloquialism for elite high-society types) or possibly "highfalutin" — context is Dexter Ave Baptist's high-society membership becoming friends with Ralph Abernathy after years of class-feud; preserved as common-noun

"we were not involved in iconic leadership"; canonical SNCC anti-charismatic-leader principle; speaker-originating idiom

Raw text exact: "Mrs. Odessa, Williams later became ready. They all jumped into gear to rescue and provide refuge for the Freedom Riders." — Pass 2's "Wilson" guess unverified; "ready" may be literal "ready/active" or Whisper-garble for a married name (Reedy / Riley / Ready); demoting prior P2.40 confidence

"And doing my administration, they were my personal bodyguards. Okay. You know, and where was munitions chat? Oh yeah, we knew all of that." — ROTC bodyguard context discussing where weapons were stored; "munitions chat" is likely Whisper for "munitions chest" or "munitions cache" (consonant approximation)

pending_context.whisper: "to keep this movement thing alive, Warren Hamilton and Benny James"
pending_context.candidate_correction: (Warren Hamilton + Benny James — next-cohort TIAL student body presidents)
pending_context.notes: "we got to line up presidents for the next four years, which we did, Warren Hamilton and Benny James and, you know, at all." — Patton's named successors as TIAL/Tuskegee student body president; preserved as speaker-originating until canonical Tuskegee-student-government verification

"And this Montgomery community turned on us, the boogeys, and Dexter. That's a whole nother story." — Patton's Black-vernacular "boogies/bougies" (bourgeoisie), same as her earlier "bougie doctor" P2.82; "boogeys" is Whisper for "bourgies" / "bougies"

"Yes. At the consternation of the black middle class bourgeoisie, he was dog skin in you. Lucius Amerson, there was a book written by his son." — context: bourgeoisie's reaction to dark-skinned Lucius Amerson winning Macon County sheriff election 1966; "dog skin in you" may be Whisper for "dark-skinned, you know" or vernacular "dog-gone you"; preserved low-confidence for adversarial

pass9_metadata.credit_formula: match_factor * min(0.10, max(0, 0.02 + 0.001*healed - 0.005*fails)); credit = 0 if healed=0 or match<0.3
